name: Spontaneous baryogenesis (Cohen-Kaplan)
summary: A scalar field phi with derivative coupling (d_mu phi) J^mu_B to the baryon current introduces an effective chemical potential mu_B = dot{phi} for baryons in the rolling phase. B-violating processes (sphalerons, GUT decays) in equilibrium then generate a baryon asymmetry n_B / s ~ mu_B / T at decoupling. The mechanism produces baryon asymmetry without explicit CP violation: the rolling scalar provides the C and CP violation spontaneously through its time-evolving background.

addresses_puzzles.note: Predicts asymmetry eta_B ~ (dot{phi} / M) (T_decoup / M_pl) where M is the coupling-suppression scale. Achievable for phi a Goldstone of broken U(1)_B (with M ~ f, dot{phi} ~ f H) or phi the inflaton (with M ~ M_pl). Closely related to gravitational baryogenesis (Davoudiasl et al. 2004) where phi = R Ricci scalar.

introduces.note: Coupling scale M and scalar potential V(phi). If phi is the inflaton or a quintessence field, no new scalar required.

history: Cohen and Kaplan (1987), 'Thermodynamic generation of the baryon asymmetry', Phys. Lett. B 199, 251. Followup: Cohen-Kaplan (1988), Nucl. Phys. B 308, 913 - 'Spontaneous baryogenesis'. Gravitational generalization: Davoudiasl, Kitano, Kribs, Murayama, Steinhardt (2004), 'Gravitational baryogenesis', PRL 93, 201301, arXiv:hep-ph/0403019.

references.citation: Cohen and Kaplan, 'Thermodynamic generation of the baryon asymmetry', Phys. Lett. B 199, 251 (1987)
Cohen and Kaplan, 'Spontaneous baryogenesis', Nucl. Phys. B 308, 913 (1988)
